title: A Frequency-Domain Approach for Enhanced Performance and Task Flexibility in Finite-Time ILC
author: van Haren, Max
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Iterative learning control (ILC) is capable of improving the tracking performance of control systems by utilizing data from past iterations. The aim of this paper is to achieve both task flexibility, which ILC often achieves with basis functions, and the performance of frequency-domain ILC, with an intuitive design procedure. The cost function of norm optimal ILC is determined that recovers frequency-domain ILC, and consequently, the feedforward is parameterized in terms of basis functions and frequency-domain ILC. The resulting method has the performance and design procedure of frequency-domain ILC and the task flexibility of basis functions ILC, and are complimentary to each other. Validation on a benchmark example confirms the capabilities of the developed framework.

Validation on a benchmark example confirms the capabilities of the developed framework.
